citeinfo.title: S_USA.Trans_MVUM_Road
citeinfo.othercit: The Federal Register Notice (73 FR 74689) for the final travel management directives was published on December 9, 2008. The directives became effective January 8, 2009. Travel Management Rule (36 CFR 212, Subpart B, Designation of Roads, Trails, and Areas for Motor Vehicle Use). See http://www.fs.fed.us/recreation/programs/ohv/index.shtml for more information.

descript.abstract: The feature class indicates the specific types of motorized vehicles allowed on the designated routes and their seasons of use. The feature class is designed to be consistent with the Motor Vehicle Use Map (MVUM). Only roads with a SYMBOL attribute value of 1, 2, 3, 4, 11, and 12 are Forest Service System roads and contain data concerning their availability for OHV (Off Highway Vehicle) use. This data is published and refreshed on a unit by unit basis as needed. Information for each individual unit must be verified as to be consistent with the published MVUMs prior to inclusion in this data. Not every National Forest has data included in this feature class.
descript.purpose: This feature class contains National Forest System roads which have been designated as open to motorized vehicles under the Travel Management Rule (36 CFR 212, Subpart B, Designation of Roads, Trails, and Areas for Motor Vehicle Use). Routes not designated for motor vehicle use (such as non-motorized trails, single-purpose roads and trails, unauthorized roads and trails, and temporary roads and trails) are not included.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

dataqual.complete: Completeness is dependent on the data collected and maintained by individual Forest Service administrative units.

horizpa.horizpar: Generally the source information used to record spatial locations are from: 1) on-screen digitizing with a georeferenced topographic or image background; or 2) GPS field collected positions.

procstep.procdesc: Forests individually manage the Road Feature Class for the geographic area they manage. Creation of this feature class from the source data is automated using a combination of FME (Feature Manipulation Engine software) Workbenches, Python scripts, and ArcObject tools.

enttyp.enttypd: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Route Control Number

attrdomv.udom: CN

attr.attrdef: The local route number for a road. The official identifier of the route. This item identifies routes within a Forest Service unit.

attrdomv.udom: ID

attr.attrdef: Common name of the road

attrdomv.udom: Name

attr.attrdef: Beginning measure point of the route segment, expressed in miles.

attrdomv.udom: Miles

attr.attrdef: Ending measure point of the route segment, expressed in miles. The EMP is the BMP plus the measured length of the route.

attrdomv.udom: Miles

attr.attrdef: Length of the segment, in miles (calculated from BMP - EMP). BMP and EMP are generally derived from field measurements and so may be different than the GIS_MILES attribute which is calculated by the GIS for each segment.

attrdomv.udom: miles

attr.attrdef: Shape length of a feature in miles, calculated by converting the data from Decimal Degrees to meters with the formula LengthMeters * 0.000621469277.

attrdomv.udom: This field is calculated by reprojecting the feature to North_America_Albers_Equal_Area_Conic projection and putting the feature through a function to change it from meters to miles.

attr.attrdef: Values are used to show which symbol shall represent the route on the Forest Service Motor Vehicle Use Map. Values 1-12 are only used for roads in the Forest Service road system. Values 13 and 15 are other roads that may be used to access National Forest roads or used to show highways and other locally significant roads.

edom.edomvd: Symbol has not yet been assigned
Roads open to all Vehicles, Yearlong
Roads open to all Vehicles, Seasonal
Roads open to highway legal vehicles only, Yearlong
Roads open to highway legal vehicles only, Seasonal

attr.attrdef: The description of the symbol that represents the route on the Forest Service Motor Vehicle Use Map.

The legal right to control or regulate use of a transportation facility. Jurisdiction requires authority, but not necessarily ownership. The authority to construct or maintain a road may be derived from fee title, an easement, an agreement, or some other similar method.

edom.edomvd: Needs to be researched and resolved
State Lands organizations (Department of Natural Resources)
Town, Township, Municipal Agency (City or other local civil government)

attr.attrdef: The maintenance level currently assigned to the road considering current needs, road condition, budget constraints and environmental concerns. Operational Maintenance Level defines the level to which the road is currently being maintained.

edom.edomvd: Assigned to roads that provide a high degree of user comfort and convenience.
Assigned to roads that provide a moderate degree of user comfort and convenience at moderate travel speeds.
Assigned to roads open for and maintained for travel by a prudent driver in a standard passenger car.
Assigned to roads open for use by high clearance vehicles.
Assigned to roads that have been placed in storage (> one year) between intermittent uses. Basic custodial maintenance is performed. Road is closed to vehicular traffic.

attr.attrdef: The wearing course; usually designed to resist skidding, traffic abrasion, and the disintegrating effects of weather.

edom.edomvd: Imported or processed material (pit run, select borrow, or admixture added to the surface soil and compacted)
Template has been cleared and rough shape completed but can’t beused until frozen conditions exist.
Built up surface of asphalt emulsion and aggregate, not a dust palliative.

attr.attrdef: A network of travel ways serving a common need or purpose, managed by an entity with the authority to finance, build, operate and maintain the routes.

edom.edomvd: An existing road whose purpose and need has yet to be determined.

Roads authorized by contract, permit, lease, other written authorization, or emergency operation not intended to be part of the forest transportation system and not necessary for longterm resource management. (36 CFR 212.1)
State roads other than those on the state highway system
Other Federal Agency (such as BLM, NPS, BIA, etc.)
A road not needed for long term management of National Forest Resources (FSM 7712.01 B(2), (36 CFR 212))

A forest road other than a road which has been authorized by a legally documented right-of-way held by a State, county or other local public road authority. (36 CFR 212.1, 36 CFR 251.51, 36 CFR 261.2). Previously known as FDR -FOREST DEVELOPMENT ROAD
Town, Township, Municipal Agency (City or other local civil government)

attr.attrdef: Can be used to easily determine if a FS road is open for public travel all year or if it has a seasonal restriction.

edom.edomvd: Open for only a portion of the year

attr.attrdef: Open to use of passenger vehicles such as sedans, and other typical low clearance vehicles less than 10,000 GVW licensed to operate on public roads
Dates open for travel by Passenger Vehicles

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open to use of a vehicle described as 'All sport utility vehicles (SUV), light trucks, motorcycles and other highway legal vehicles designed for operation on rough terrain'.
Dates route is open for High Clearance Vehicle travel

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open to use of a vehicle described as "All motor vehicles greater than 10,000 pounds GVW that are designed, used, or maintained primarily for the transportation of property or equipment, such as lowboys, log trucks, chip trucks, end dumps and fire trucks licensed to operate on public roads."
Dates open for travel by trucks.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open to use of a vehicle described as 'All motor vehicles designed for carrying more than 10 passengers and greater than 10,000 pounds GVW licensed to operate on public roads'.
Dates open for travel by buses.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open for use of a vehicle described as 'All motor vehicles that are self-contained living quarters on wheels licensed to operate on public roads'.
Dates route is open for travel by motor homes.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open to use of a vehicle described as 'Four wheel drive vehicles greater than 50 inches in width'.
Dates route is open for travel by four wheel drive greater than 50 inches in width.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open to use of a vehicle described as 'Two wheel drive vehicles greater than 50 inches in width'.
Dates route is open for travel by two wheel drive vehicles greater than 50 inches in width.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open to use of Off Highway Vehicles (OHV) described as 'OHVs greater than 50 inches in width operating on tracks, including SUVs or utility vehicles with track conversion kits'.

edom.edomvd: Open to tracked OHV greater than 50"

attr.attrdef: Dates route is open for travel by tracked Off Highway Vehicles (OHV) greater than 50 inches in width.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open to use of Off Highway Vehicles (OHV) described as 'Other OHVs greater than 50 inches in width that are not wheeled or tracked'.

edom.edomvd: Open to other OHV greater than 50"

attr.attrdef: Dates route is open for travel by other Off Highway Vehicles (OHV) greater than 50 inches in width.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open to use of a vehicle described as 'OHVs less than or equal to 50 incles in width with three or more low-pressure tires, handle-bar steering and a seat designed to be straddled by the operator'.
Dates route is open for travel by ATVs

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open for use of a vehicle described as 'Two-wheeled vehicles on which the two wheels are inline, not side-by-side'.
Dates route is open for travel by motorcycles.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open to use of a vehicle described as 'Other wheeled OHVs less than or equal to 50 inches in width. Includes balancing scooters'.
Dates route is open for travel by Other Wheeled OHVs.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open to use of a vehicle described as 'An OHV less than or equal to 50 inches in width operating on tracks'. Includes ATVs with track conversion kits and snowmobiles when not operating over snow.

edom.edomvd: Open to tracked OHV less than 50"

attr.attrdef: Dates route is open for travel by tracked OHVs less than or equal to 50 inches in width.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Open to use of a vehicle described as 'Other OHVs less than or equal to 50 inches in width that are not considered to be ATVs or motorcycles and are not wheeled or tracked'.

edom.edomvd: Open to other OHV less than 50"

attr.attrdef: Dates route is open for travel by other OHVs less than or equal to 50 inches in width.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Administrative unit. The Forest Service unit where the route segment physical resides or the primary Forest Service unit served by the route segment.

attrdomv.udom: Format: RRFFDD where RR is the 2-digit code for the Region, FF is the 2-digit code for the Forest, and DD is the 2-digit code for the Ranger District

attr.attrdef: The number identifies to which organization the asset (routes, features, land units, etc.) belongs and acts as a filter that controls what asset records a user can see and edit. It uses a similar numbering method as Managing Organization (region/forest).

attrdomv.udom: The database record was generated from the National Forest database indicated by the Security_ID. Security_ID is composed of the Region and Forest numbers (i.e., RRFF). It does not necessarily indicate the Administrative Unit (ADMINORG) that the road is assigned to.

attr.attrdef: The name of the Ranger District that the manages the road segment.

attrdomv.udom: The name of the US Forest District that administers the land.

attr.attrdef: The name of the National Forest that manages the road segment.

attrdomv.udom: The name of the US Forest that administers the land

attr.attrdef: An optional field for holding a route name or number for labeling purposes if the ID or Name field is not appropriate.

attrdomv.udom: Route name or number

attr.attrdef: Descriptive name for the road classification as shown in the map legend

edom.edomv: Gravel Road, Suitable for Passenger Car
edom.edomvd: Gravel Road, Suitable for Passenger Car

edom.edomv: Dirt Road, Suitable for Passenger Car
edom.edomvd: Dirt Road, Suitable for Passenger Car

edom.edomv: Road, Not Maintained for Passenger Car
edom.edomvd: Road, Not Maintained for Passenger Car

attr.attrdef: Current physical state of being of the route segment

edom.edomvd: Planned route identified in a completed NEPA document, with a Record of Decision.
A route that was no longer needed and has been removed from service.
A route that was no longer needed and has been converted to another use

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.

attrdomv.udom: Coordinates defining the features.

attr.attrdef: A field of type UUID (Universal Unique Identifier) in which values are automatically assigned by the geodatabase when a row is created. The GlobalID field is necessary for maintaining object uniqueness across replicas. All feature classes and tables participating in one-way or two-way replication must contain the GlobalID field. This field is not editable and is automatically populated when it is added for existing data.

attrdomv.udom: Unique identifier.

attr.attrdef: TA_SYMBOL

attrdomv.udom: TA_SYMBOL

attr.attrdef: An e-bike equipped with a motor that provides assistance only when the rider is pedaling and that ceases to provide assistance when the e-bike reaches the speed of 20 miles per hour.

Open for use to a vehicle described as: An e-bike equipped with a motor that provides assistance only when the rider is pedaling and that ceases to provide assistance when the e-bike reaches the speed of 20 miles per hour.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: An e-bike equipped with a motor that may be used exclusively to propel the e-bike and that ceases to provide assistance when the e-bike reaches the speed of 20 miles per hour.

Open for use to a vehicle described as: An e-bike equipped with a motor that may be used exclusively to propel the e-bike and that ceases to provide assistance when the e-bike reaches the speed of 20 miles per hour.

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: An e-bike equipped with a motor that provides assistance only when the rider is pedaling and that ceases to provide assistance when the e-bike reaches the speed of 28 miles per hour.

Open for use to a vehicle described as: An e-bike equipped with a motor that provides assistance only when the rider is pedaling and that ceases to provide assistance when the e-bike reaches the speed of 28 miles per hour

attrdomv.udom: Calendar date range formatted as MM/DD-MM/DD

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
